messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Build a dashboard module with auth and an API, with unit tests and database stubs. Include modules A and B.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Build a dashboard module with auth and an API, with unit tests and database stubs. Include modules A and B.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "architecture-design",
      "title": "Design overall architecture",
      "description": "Define component boundaries, data flow, authentication model, API contract, and how Modules A and B fit into the dashboard module.",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "A written architecture document (diagram + responsibilities) approved by team that specifies API endpoints, auth flows, database abstractions, and how Modules A and B integrate."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-stubs",
      "title": "Create database stubs and interfaces",
      "description": "Implement in-memory or mock database interfaces and schema stubs for users, dashboard data, and Module A/B data access.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "architecture-design"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Reusable stub implementations and interface definitions exist; unit tests can run against stubs without needing a real DB."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-service",
      "title": "Implement authentication service",
      "description": "Develop auth module providing login, token issuance/validation, and permission checks, wired to the user datastore stub.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "architecture-design",
        "database-stubs"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "Auth endpoints and token validation are implemented and can be exercised in tests; unauthorized requests are denied per spec."
    },
    {
      "id": "module-a-implementation",
      "title": "Implement Module A",
      "description": "Build Module A backend logic, endpoints (if any), and connect to database stubs following the architecture.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "architecture-design",
        "database-stubs"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Module A functions are implemented with unit tests passing against stubs and documented API/usage."
    },
    {
      "id": "module-b-implementation",
      "title": "Implement Module B",
      "description": "Build Module B backend logic, endpoints (if any), and connect to database stubs following the architecture.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "architecture-design",
        "database-stubs"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Module B functions are implemented with unit tests passing against stubs and documented API/usage."
    },
    {
      "id": "api-endpoints",
      "title": "Develop dashboard API endpoints",
      "description": "Implement REST/GraphQL endpoints for the dashboard, including routes that surface Module A/B data and enforce auth checks.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "architecture-design",
        "auth-service",
        "module-a-implementation",
        "module-b-implementation"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "All specified API endpoints are implemented, respect auth rules, return expected payloads, and have integration tests using stubs."
    },
    {
      "id": "unit-tests",
      "title": "Write comprehensive unit tests",
      "description": "Create unit tests for auth, API handlers, Module A and B logic, and database stubs to achieve targeted coverage.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "database-stubs",
        "auth-service",
        "module-a-implementation",
        "module-b-implementation",
        "api-endpoints"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Automated test suite runs in CI, with passing tests and stated coverage thresholds met for the dashboard module and submodules."
    },
    {
      "id": "integration-and-deployment",
      "title": "Integrate components and prepare deployment",
      "description": "Integrate dashboard, auth, API, modules A/B and stubs; add basic deployment config and documentation for running locally and in CI.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "api-endpoints",
        "unit-tests"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "End-to-end demo runs locally using stubs; deployment scripts/config exist and a README describes how to run dashboard with auth and API."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 8,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down decomposition: start with a high-level architecture, create reusable DB stubs, implement core services (auth), then implement Modules A and B, expose them via API endpoints, add unit tests, and finish with integration and deployment. Dependencies enforce order and testability."
}
